knimeNode.name: Spark Unpivot(Hyim)
knimeNode.shortDescription: Transforms a Spark DataFrame/RDD from wide format to long format by converting selected value columns into variable/value rows.

intro.p: This node performs an unpivot (melt) operation on a Spark DataFrame/RDD. It transforms wide-format data into long-format data by converting selected value columns into two new columns: a variable column (containing the original column names) and a value column (containing the original cell values).
Retained columns (e.g. ID columns) are kept as-is and repeated for each value column that is unpivoted.
For example, a table with columns [ID, Q1, Q2, Q3] where Q1-Q3 are value columns would be transformed into [ID, variable, value] with one row per original value column.
The node uses Spark's stack() SQL function internally.

tab.name: Retained Columns
tab.option: Select the columns that should be retained (kept as-is) in the output. These are typically ID or key columns. Retained columns will be repeated for each unpivoted value column row.

tab.name: Value Columns
tab.option: Select the columns that should be unpivoted (melted) into rows. Each selected column will generate one row per input row in the output, with the column name stored in the variable column and the cell value stored in the value column.

tab.name: Options
tab.option: The name of the output column that will contain the original column names. Default: "variable".
The name of the output column that will contain the original cell values. Default: "value".
If checked, rows where the value is missing (null) will be omitted from the output.

ports.outPort: Spark DataFrame/RDD in long format with variable and value columns.
